name: Molinos de la Rivera de los Arroyos — running circular from Moral de Sayago
name_original: Molinos de la Rivera de los Arroyos — running ciruclar desde Moral de Sayago
region_intro: Jogging around Sayago offers diverse landscapes, from well-maintained greenways to natural terrain. The region features a mix of river valleys, rolling hills, and rocky outcrops, providing varied running experiences. Paths often follow historical routes and offer views of ancient settlements and natural features. The terrain includes both smooth surfaces and gravel paths, suitable for different running preferences. Best jogging routes around Sayago The most popular jogging route is Mirador de las Escaleras y ermita de Santa Cruz — running round trip desde Fermoselle, a 3.9 miles (6.3 km) trail that takes about 45 minutes to complete, offering scenic viewpoints and historical landmarks. Another top favourite among local runners is The Rivera de los Molinos de Brandilanes — running circular from Castro de Alcañices, a moderate 5.4 miles (8.6 km) path that follows river courses and passes old mills. Local runners also love the Duero Great Meander Trail, a 5.1 miles (8.3 km) trail leading through river valleys and offering views of the Duero, often completed in about 55 minutes. Jogging around Sayago is defined by greenways like the Vía Verde de la Plata, gravel paths around Teso Santo, and routes near historical rocky hills. The network offers options for easy jogs and more strenuous runs across different ability levels. The routes in Sayago are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 600 runners have used komoot to explore Sayago's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Sayago?
A: Sayago offers a wide selection of running routes, with over 45 different trails to explore. These range from easy jogs to more challenging runs, catering to various fitness levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on jogging trails in Sayago?
A: The terrain in Sayago is quite varied. You'll find well-maintained greenways like the &lt;strong&gt;Vía Verde de la Plata&lt;/strong&gt;, ideal for smooth running. There are also gravel paths, especially around areas like &lt;strong&gt;Teso Santo&lt;/strong&gt;, and routes that navigate rocky hills and river valleys. This mix ensures diverse running experiences.

Q: Are there running routes suitable for beginners in Sayago?
A: Yes, Sayago has several easy running routes perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed jog. There are 8 routes specifically categorized as easy, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1026637242/molinos-de-la-rivera-de-los-arroyos-running-circular-from-moral-de-sayago" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molinos de la Rivera de los Arroyos — running circular from Moral de Sayago&lt;/a&gt;, which is just over 3.4 km.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Sayago?
A: The running routes in Sayago are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 4.9 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 600 runners have explored the region, often praising the varied terrain and scenic views.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints or natural attractions along the running paths in Sayago?
A: Absolutely. Many routes offer stunning views. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1026443509/mirador-de-las-escaleras-y-ermita-de-santa-cruz-running-round-trip-desde-fermoselle" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de las Escaleras y ermita de Santa Cruz — running round trip desde Fermoselle&lt;/a&gt; route provides scenic viewpoints. You can also find natural attractions like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4791099" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Staircase Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5442598" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de las Barrancas&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1002046" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;São João das Arribas Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find historical landmarks while jogging in Sayago?
A: Yes, Sayago's trails often weave through areas rich in history. The &lt;strong&gt;Vía Verde de la Plata&lt;/strong&gt; passes near historical sites like the 15th-century Convent of Santa María del Soto. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5437964" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Santa Cruz&lt;/a&gt; is a notable landmark found along some routes.

Q: Are there any routes that follow river courses or pass by old mills?
A: Yes, several routes in Sayago highlight the region's natural and historical connection to water. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1026430449/the-rivera-de-los-molinos-de-brandilanes-running-circular-from-castro-de-alcanices" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Rivera de los Molinos de Brandilanes — running circular from Castro de Alcañices&lt;/a&gt; trail follows river courses and passes old mills. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1026450115/duero-great-meander-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Duero Great Meander Trail&lt;/a&gt; also offers views of the Duero river.

Q: Are there any waterfalls to see on the running trails in Sayago?
A: Yes, for those interested in natural water features, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5442792" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abelón Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is a highlight in the region, which can be incorporated into or viewed from some running excursions.

Q: Are there circular running routes available in Sayago?
A: Many of Sayago's running paths are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1026430449/the-rivera-de-los-molinos-de-brandilanes-running-circular-from-castro-de-alcanices" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Rivera de los Molinos de Brandilanes — running circular from Castro de Alcañices&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1026637242/molinos-de-la-rivera-de-los-arroyos-running-circular-from-moral-de-sayago" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molinos de la Rivera de los Arroyos — running circular from Moral de Sayago&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Sayago?
A: Sayago offers good running conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. While specific seasonal conditions can vary, the diverse landscape means there are suitable routes for different times of the year.

Q: Are there any running routes near lakes or reservoirs in Sayago?
A: Yes, the region features beautiful water bodies. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3539630" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almendra Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; is a significant lake in the area, and you might find routes that offer views or access to its shores, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6065885" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salce Beach – Almendra Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging running routes in Sayago?
A: For experienced runners seeking a challenge, Sayago offers several difficult routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10411254" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Sendim e Atenor&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 17.3 km trail with significant elevation gain, providing a strenuous workout.
tips: Beautiful picture that the Calabaza stream gives us next to the long-distance path (GR-14) Senda del Duero. As we move away from the town of Moral de Sayago, the images, in my opinion, become more beautiful.

Departing from the town of Moral de Sayago, the trail runs along the Senda del Duero Nature Trail. In a detour from it is the Molino Llagalgüey. Its presence and the fact that it has been rehabilitated adds another element of interest to a riverbank that, due to its natural characteristics, deserves to be visited. During the tour we will find more mills that humans have built taking advantage of the Arroyo de los Arroyos.
Nice path that runs near Moral de Sayago accompanied by a multitude of holm oaks and walls that give us some beautiful prints.

waypoints.name: Holm Oaks Along the Path Near Moral de Sayago
